lines.text: Central Perk, Gary, Phoebe, Monica, and Chandler are there.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: (To Phoebe) Would you like some more coffee, baby-doll?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm fine, thanks.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Monica) Yeah, see, I can't pull of baby-doll can I?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No. I think we learned that from the sugarlips incident. I'm gonna get some tea.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.
Monica leaves and Chandler moves to talk to Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hiya doin' pumpkin?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Nope. (Chandler nods in agreement.)
[Cut to Gary and Monica at the counter.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So it looks like it's going really well for you two, huh?

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: I know, really well. In fact, I'm gonna ask Phoebe to move in with me.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (shocked) Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: What do you, what do you think?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I think that is so great! When are you gonna ask her?

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Tonight, but don't say anything. Okay?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I swear, I promise. I promise. Oh my God, I'm so excited! {And I just can't hide it! I'm about to lose control and I think I like it! Sorry, just couldn't resist it.} All right, listen let me tell you, do not get her flowers. Okay? Because y'know, she cries when they die, and there's the whole funeral...

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: (To Phoebe) I'll see you after work sweetie. (Kisses her.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. Bye!
Gary exits and Monica rejoins them.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So, what movie should we see?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (sitting down) Gary's gonna ask you to move in with him!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?! Really?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He just told me at the counter. He made me promise not to tell, but I couldn't hold it in any longer!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can't believe this!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (terrified) Right, because it's fast. Because, it's so fast. It's fast!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Relax! It's Phoebe! Not you!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh! Good for you Pheebs, way to go! (Breathes a sigh of relief)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, but it is fast. Isn't it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohhhh!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, I like him a lot but I don't think I'm ready for this!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, what are you gonna do?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don't know. I'll just handle it-I'll ask you to talk to him!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Me?! Why me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Because you are so afraid of commitment! You talk to him, make him scared like you! Make him a...man!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'll try, but I'm not sure what good it would do, y'know? Because I'm a lot less afraid of commitment than I used to be.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That is so sweet! (She kisses him and turns to add some sugar to her tea.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Phoebe, behind Monica's back) Still terrified, I'll take care of it. No problem. (When Monica turns back he smiles and kisses her, when she turns away he nods that he'll do it to Phoebe.)
Opening Credits
Monica and Rachel's, Joey and Ross are playing catch with a little foam globe.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Ross, is uh, is Staten Island really an island?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh-huh, that's why they call it Staten Island.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ohhh. I thought it was like Long Island.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (he catches the ball and pauses, staring at Joey in disbelief) Also an island.
The game resumes.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, what time is it?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (looks at his watch) 2:17.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow! You realize that we've been throwing this ball, without dropping it, for like an hour?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Are you serious?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. I realized it about a half-hour ago but I didn't want to say anything 'cause I didn't want to jinx it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! We are pretty good at this! Hey! We totally forgot about lunch!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I-I, I think that's the first time I ever missed a meal! (Checks his pants.) Yeah, my pants are a little loose!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Hey, you guys...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Is Monica here?

lines.speaker: Joey and Ross
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right listen umm, I just bought something I'm not sure she's gonna like it, and it's gonna seem a little crazy, but this is something that I wanted since I was a little girl.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You bought Shawn Cassidy!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Noo! I wish! Okay, you ready?

lines.speaker: Joey and Ross
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay! (She goes and gets her surprise and when she returns with it, Ross stares in shock.) Check it out!
Joey turns and looks at quite possibly one of the ugliest pets that you can possibly buy on the planet. Rachel has bought herself a hairless cat. Yep, a hairless cat! Joey and Ross start to get sick.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What-what is it?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What the hell is that?!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's a, it's a cat!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That, is not a cat! {I have to agree with Joey on this one.}

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes it is!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why is it inside out?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Excuse me! But this is a purebred, show-quality Sphinx cat!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: How much did you pay for that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, it was a little extravagant, but I a pretty good deal.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah? How much?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: A thousand bucks.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ON A CAT??!!!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's not a cat!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right listen ball boys! My grandmother had one of these when I was a little girl and it was the sweetest thing! I mean it was so cute, it would sit in my lap and purr all day long, and I would drag a shoestring on the ground and he would chase it!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Free cats do that too, y'know. {Which reminds me, if I might get a little political here, support your local animal shelter. Pet shops are not the place to buy dogs and cats from, you get a much better deal from the shelter, plus they probably won't die on you in a week and a half. If you want a leash, go to the pet shop. If you want the dog for that leash, go to the shelter and save it's life. Now back to regularly scheduled programming.}

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's not a cat!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ugh! Look you guys, I'm really excited about this! Okay? I don't care what you think! I'm gonna go set up a little litter box for Mrs. Whiskerson. (They both glare at her.) Well, what am I gonna call her? Fluffy?!
Rachel goes into the bathroom as the guys continue throwing the ball.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Joey) Hey, you wanna get something to eat or uh, do you wanna see how long we can throw this ball back and forth? Huh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhh, the ball thing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Ross, wouldn't it be great if we could go two straight hours without dropping it?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uhh, yeah it would! Let's do it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay!
They throw the ball back and forth once.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (catches the ball) Uh-oh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I have to pee. And Rachel's in the bathroom!
[Cut to Chandler and Joey's, Ross is seen throwing the ball into the bathroom, presumably where Joey is currently using the facilities.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Man, I didn't think we were gonna make it!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know! (Looks at the ball in his hands.) Don't switch hands, okay?

the 5th Precinct, Gary's precinct, Chandler has come to talk to him about commitment. And as he's walking through the door he notices a couple of "Ladies of the night" sitting there. (If you know what I mean.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey ladies! What are you in here for? (Laughs at his joke.)

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Hey Chandler, what are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Gary, I'm here to report a crime.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It is a crime that you and I don't spend more time together.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: (laughs, then suddenly serious) What's up?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I heard that you thinking about asking Phoebe to move in with you and I thought maybe, we should have a talk. Man to uh, me.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Sure. Okay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh, are you crazy? Are you insane? If you live with Phoebe, you two are gonna be y'know, live-living together!

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Yeah, I-I considered that. I just know it would make me happy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You mean scared.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: No, I mean happy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Scared? Happy?

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Chandler, what-what are you doing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I am trying to open your eyes, my man! Don't you see, if you lived with Phoebe she's always gonna be there. You're gonna get home, she's there. You go to bed, she's there. You wake up and oh yes, she's there!

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: I know! I can't wait!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Were you're parents happy, or something?

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Listen Chandler, the way I see it is that I was lucky enough to find someone that I really love. I just-I wanna be around her as much as I can.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow, y'know when you say it, it doesn't sound so scary.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: So you know what I'm talking about, right?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I think I do! Y'know what? You move in with her! You move in with her right now! Maybe I should in with Monica!

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: No, it's too soon for you guys.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (pause) Yeah, you're right about that.
Chandler and Joey's, Ross and Joey are still playing catch.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ...now when they found the remains of the Mesozoic Mastodon they discovered what appeared to be the remains of a Paleozoic Amphibian in its jaws! How did it get there?!

{Y'know, sometimes I think the script writers throw in a line like that to try to trip me up. But it won't work. I'll always have the last laugh! <manical_laugh.wav> Okay, so maybe I'm a little deluded, it's probably just my spellchecker. But, I must admit I did get Mesozoic and Paleozoic on the first attempt. Yay me! Anyhoo...}

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Maybe this should be more of a quiet game.
{Oh, all right!  Geez, I can't have any fun!}

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering) Hey guys!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, I left my watch on the counter last night. (Goes to the counter) It was right here, where is it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, come on, I'm-I'm late for work!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: How do you know? You don't have a watch.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Guys, could you please just stop throwing the ball for one minute and just help me find it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I don't know.... Yeah, can't do it.
He throws it back to Ross, but it's intercepted by Monica and the guys both scream in horror.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Monica, whatever you do, do not drop that ball!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, we haven't dropped it in... (Looks to Ross.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: 2 hours, 27 minutes.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really?!
[Time lapse, Monica has joined in and is calling to get out of work.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (on phone, faking she's sick) I-I'm not gonna be able to make it into work today, I don't feel very good. (Joey makes a high throw and Monica has to catch it way over her head.) (Not sounding sick) Yes!! (Realizes what she just did.) (Sounding sick again) Wow! Uh, for a second there I thought I was really better, but I'm not. (Hangs up and keeps throwing the ball.)
Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey Rach!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Check it out! Almost 3 hours without droppin' it!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, wow! Congratulations, that's quite a waste of time.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rach?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You have scratches all over you, what happened? (Rachel's arms are covered with scratches.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, it's my cat.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (shocked) What?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah, I got a cat.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't want a cat!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, don't worry, it's not a cat.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You guys this cat is nothing like my grandmother's cat. I mean, it's not sweet, it's not cute, I even dragged that little string on the ground, and it just flipped out and scratched the hell out of me. And I swear, I know this sounds crazy, but every time this cat hisses at me I know it's saying, "Rachel!"

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Doesn't sound as crazy as paying a thousand dollars for a cat.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (To Rachel) What?! You paid a thousand dollars for a cat when you owe me 300!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I was gonna let you play with it.
Central Perk, Phoebe is drinking coffee as Chandler enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hmm, did you talk to Gary about the moving in thing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes I did, and I think you should do it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: He's a great guy, y'know? And he loves you a lot, you are a very lucky lady.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You are useless! Freaking out about commitment is the one thing you can do! The one thing! And you can't even do that right! God!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm sorry. (Pause) If you ask me, I'd move in with him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh!! God! (Gary enters and she sees him) Ooh! (To Chandler) Get out of here, good for nothing.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Hey Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey Gar!

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: (To Phoebe) Hi sweetie. (Kisses her.) Hi, can I talk to you for a second?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! Okay. (They move to the couch.)

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: You look very pretty today.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thanks! Okay. (They sit down.)

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Here's the thing.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Y'know I really want to move this relationship forward.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-hmm.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Because if you're not moving forward, y'know you're just moving backwards.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No that's not true. If you're not moving forward, you're just staying still. And staying still is good. Watch this. (She stays still for a brief second.)

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Phoebe?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: I want you to move in with me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That is so sweet. But don't you think it's a little too soon? I mean there's so much we don't know about each other.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: (disappointed) Oh. Oh-oh-okay, I get it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I just-I don't want us to jump into something we're not ready for.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: (disappointed more) Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I really don't want to mess up what we have. I'm just-I'm worried it's gonna be a big mistake.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: (on the verge of tears) Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Which is why my answer is yes!

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: (suddenly happy) Really?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh! (They hug.) I'm so...happy. (She's not happy.)
Commercial Break
Chandler and Joey's, Joey, Ross, and Monica are still playing catch, with Chandler looking on. Joey throws the ball to Monica who catches it and whips it at Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (catching the ball) Monica! Stop throwing it so hard! We're on the same team!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Four hours? You guys have been doing this for four hours?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's right baby.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, let me in. (He jumps off of the counter to join in the game.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (jumping in front of him) No-no! Don't do it! Don't!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He's a dropper!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah, that's right!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm not a dropper!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's really a uh-uh three person game, y'know?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's throwing and catching!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right. (He gently tosses the ball to Chandler who catches it.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh! Oh! That's so hard. (Starts to juggle the ball, but loses control and almost drops it and hands it to Monica.)
Rachel enters with the "cat" and the chick and the duck start to get riled up.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa-whoa you guys, it's not a cat!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my-Oh good God!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (she's wearing an oven mitt to protect her hand) I give up you guys, I don't know what I'm going to do with this thing!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Baking it didn't help, huh?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So, why don't you just take it back to where you got it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I tried! They won't take her back.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Maybe that's because she's a minion of the anti-Christ.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rach, why won't they take it back?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, they said would but they would only give me store credit. I mean, what am I going to do, get a thousand regular cats?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Look, if you want you can keep it at our place until you find out what to do with it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No Mon that's not the point. I'm out a thousand dollars, I'm all scratched up, and I'm stuck with this stupid cat that looks like a hand! (Storms out.)
Monica sneezes.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, the cat's made my eyes water! Don't-don't throw it to me! My vision's been compromised!! (Quickly grabs a tissue to wipe her eyes.) Oh God! Okay. Okay. It's okay. Man, that was close.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, you almost overreacted to something.

lines.speaker: Phoebe and Gary
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: We have great news!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We're moving in together! Isn't it great! Yay!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Congratulations! Congrats!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know, I'm so excited!

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: So am I!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, you're not more excited than I am! No way! I'm the most excited!

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Okay, I'll see you at the station later.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, yeah, I'll see you later! Don't forget about the moving in!

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: All right.
Phoebe closes the door behind him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So you're moving in with him. What happened?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I couldn't tell him no. He got so sad. Maybe it'll be all right. I do really like him a lot and probably do it eventually anyway and plus, think of all the money I'll save on stamps.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why, do you write him a lot?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, I just heard when people live together, they split the cost of stamps. Don't they?

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Yeah! That's right. Yeah-yeah! Yeah!
Rachel enters with the cat, wearing the oven mitt, and startles Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry, the oven mitts really freaked me out.
Gary's precinct, Phoebe is entering.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Hey, honey! Okay, so did you find any apartments? Anything in Brooklyn Heights?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, nothing.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Oh really?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Nothing at all?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, as soon as something opens up we'll move right in. Unless it doesn't have a pool, I need a pool. (Turns away from him.)

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Phoebe, can I talk to you for a second?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh!
He takes her into one of those typical interrogation rooms you see on TV and in the movies. Which is really appropriate here, since this is a TV show. What are the odds of that?

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Take a seat. You okay? You feeling all right? (Closes the door and takes off his coat.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (sits down) Yeah, I feel great. 'Cause we're moving in together.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: So you uh, you checked the paper for listings in Brooklyn Heights, right? You-you checked the Post?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, uh-huh, there was nothing. (Pause) Can I get some water?

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: In a minute. You-you checked today's Post?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, yeah! Today's.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: 'Cause uh, this is today's Post (produces one from the other chair) and uh, these are the listings I found. (Points) Brooklyn Heights, two bedroom. (Points) Brooklyn Heights, one bedroom. (Points) Brooklyn Heights, (points) Brooklyn Heights, (points) Brooklyn Heights!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (looks at the paper) Are these for rent! I thought people were just bragging!

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Let me tell you what I think might be going on. (Phoebe looks down in shame.) No-no-no, don't look at the table. Look at me. (Points to his eyes and she does so) Okay, I think somebody asked someone to move in with them. And I think someone said, "Yes" but now she's having doubts because things are moving to fast for someone. Does that sound at all possible to you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes. Yes! Fine! I am someone! You want me to say it? I have doubts! (Pause) I'm sorry! (Puts her head down.)

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Phoebe...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Phoebe, it's okay that you feel this way. I mean it is soon. And there's a lot of things we don't know about each other, and I just figure that everything I really like. And the things I don't know, I get to learn about at someplace with both our names on the mailbox.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's so sweet.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Sweethart, but none of that matters if it's too soon for you. It's fine! We don't have to move in together. I just-I want you to be happy

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Living with you would make me happy.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Phoebe, you don't have to say that.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, I really wanna live with you! I wanna move in with you!

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes. Definitely! Yes! Let's live in an apartment that we both live in! (Hugs him.)

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Oh that's great!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh wait, one sec. One sec. (Goes to the mirror) Hey you! Behind the glass! Who are you looking at! I've always wanted to say that when I was in one of these rooms, (sees the look on his face) which was never!
The street outside Central Perk, Rachel is trying to sell the cat.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Show cat! Quality show cat! Show cat! (A woman approaches.)
Woman No. 1: (looks into the box) Oh my God! What's wrong with your baby?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's not a baby! It's a cat!
Woman No. 1: Eew! It's creepy looking!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no! No! It's actually-it's very sweet. It's very sweet. Look! (Goes to pet it and it hisses at her.) Yeah, do you want it?
Woman No. 1: (laughs) No, I hate cats.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, so then what are you doing to me? Okay? Just get out of here! All right? Move on!
Another woman approaches.
Woman No. 2: Wow! What an unusual cat!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes! Thank you! Exactly! You want it?
Woman No. 2: Maybe. I was thinking about getting a cat, I was just going to go to the shelter (Good for her) but... Okay, why not?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, terrific! That'll be $2,000.
Woman No. 2: What?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, a thousand.
Woman No. 2: I thought you wanted to adopt your cat.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I do, but you're just gonna have to actually look at this as more of an investment than a cat.
Woman No. 2: Okay, yeah, I just wanted a cat. (Starts to leave.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (makes some unintelligible sound to stop her from leaving) Obviously you know how to haggle, so I'm not gonna try and take you on. Okay? So $800 and I don't call the cops because you're robbing me blind! Blind! (Covers her eyes) Just take cat, leave the money, and run away! Run away! (Uncovers her eyes and sees that the woman has fled) Damnit! (To the cat) Cat, can't you at least smile or something?! (The cat hisses at her again, it sounds like Rachel) Okay, did anybody just hear that? Anybody?

Chandler and Joey's, Joey, Ross, Monica, and now Chandler are still playing catch. The guys are exhausted and sitting around the room. Monica is still standing all pumped up and being hyper-competitive yet again. {Okay! We get it! She's competitive!! Must we see all the time?!}

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm starving!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come on guys! Suck it up! We're closing in on ten hours! It's gut-check time!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know who made you the boss? All right? We (Ross and him) invented this game!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Please! I made this game what it is.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Not fun anymore?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm still hungry!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, there's some pizza at my place, we can all eat with one hand right? Are you with me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I am!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right! Let's go! (Runs to the door.) Let's go Team Monica! (The guys all stop and stare at her) All right, we can work out the name later.
[Cut to her apartment where Rachel is sitting at the table.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rachel! What is your cat doing in one of my bowls!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's not! I'm defrosting a chicken. (Pause) Oh, I uh sold Mrs. Whiskerson.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, thank God!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Did you get your money back?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, 15 hundred dollars.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow! You made a profit!

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: (entering with the cat) I just came for the red-velvet pillow.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah, there you go. (Hands over the pillow.)

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Thanks Rachel. And-and don't forget you-you can come visit her anytime you want.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh good, great! I'll-I'll keep that in mind. (Turns and walks away.)

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: (To Ross) Hey! So what is this? Some kind of snake or something?
Gary and Phoebe's apartment, it's morning and they're both waking up in bed.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: I really like waking up with you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I like waking up with you too. (Looks out the window) Oh! It's such a beautiful morning. (Some birds are singing outside the window) Oh, I can stay here all day.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: That would be great!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We could have breakfast in bed...

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Wait, just a second.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. (He grabs his gun and shoots the bird.) Oh! Oh no.
Monica and Rachel's, Hyper-competitive Monica, Rachel, Chandler, Joey, and Ross are still playing catch. Monica is finally tiring while the rest of them are totally exhausted and virtually asleep.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right! Come on Monica! Look alive! Come on, look alive!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering) Oh good, you're all up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe! It's 6 o'clock in the morning! Why aren't you at Gary's?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah, that's over.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Come on! Gary's such a great guy! Whatever the problem is, you can work it out!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: He shot a bird!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh that is over!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: That's terrible! I'm sorry!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, are you okay?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. Yeah, I'll be alright.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh hey, here Pheebs. (He throws her the ball.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (catches it) Nah, I don't feel like playing. (She sets the ball down on the table and everyone gasps.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's okay. It's okay. Just pretend that it didn't happen! Okay? No one needs to know! I mean, Phoebe's not an official ballplayer! I mean, only official ballplayers can drop the ball!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: All right. Okay. I'm starving! (They all get up, thus officially ending the game.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, honey, wanna get some breakfast?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay! Okay, let's race! First one there wins! Ha-ha! (Runs out the door and everyone watches her leave.)
Pause

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You guys wanna eat here?

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Yeah! As long as we're here!
Ending Credits
Central Perk, everyone is there.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Man that was great! Huh? Can you believe how long we threw that ball around?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, it is amazing it lasted that long.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know. My arm is killing me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I meant with the dropper over here. (Points at Chandler.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know, how did I get this reputation as a dropper? Okay? I'm anything but a dropper. (We see various scenes of him dropping a football, a mug of coffee, the phone, an apple, a Frisbee, a record, and the final scene has a ball bouncing off of his chest. I'm not going to describe them, you'll have to see them.)
End
